evidenceBoundary: Captured external-source bytes are deterministic functional contract inputs only. Live categories execute production code without internal mocks; the no-write source smoke proves current structure or truthful unavailability, never fixed market values or recommendation correctness.

testPlanRepresentation.hashInput: Exact Markdown table row bytes followed by LF
testPlanRepresentation.ordering: Tests and TP Definition of Done items are one-based and positional within each scope

supersededPlan.reason: The five-scope plan predates the cutoff-safe market-session, CPI lineage, owner-read, final-aggregation, atomic-publication, and shared-UI amendments.

tests.description: canonicalization and versioned fingerprints ignore volatile formatting but preserve semantic order
profile eligibility rejects unsupported evidence and private fields
all current source adapters emit truthful production ToolModelRead contracts
Canary: RLDATA preserves existing publisher read semantics
Canary: RLAPP preserves honest status semantics
Canary: registry consumers retain all 18 ordered tool links
Regression: current registry freezes 17 source reads and one non-recursive final aggregator
Regression: unavailable local state and off-theme evidence never become market recommendations
Regression: registry-only participant discovery needs no scheduler inventory edit
existing full repository assertions remain green after shared-foundation changes

supersededScopes.scopeName: Bounded Per-Tool Authorship and Recommendation Lifecycle

tests.description: tool brief validation binds every recommendation to eligible owning-read evidence
deterministic compaction honors exact profile caps and stable fact priority
stable identities distinguish reaffirm modify supersede close outcome and correction events
compatible origins merge shared causes count once and conflicts remain separate
production author pool validates all 17 source outcomes with a four-call maximum
secret instruction unsafe URL fabricated evidence and illegal action outputs are redacted and rejected
duplicate responses timeouts malformed JSON and retry ceiling create one bounded outcome
Stress: seventeen changed tools never exceed four concurrent author calls or run token ceilings
Regression: all 17 source tools reach one truthful validated brief outcome before aggregation
Regression: unchanged reads carry forward and duplicate attempts create no prose or event churn
Regression: modified recommendations retain prior terms while compatible origins merge and conflicts persist
shared author and lifecycle modules introduce no product regression

supersededScopes.scopeName: Current Pointers Bounded History and Legacy Migration

tests.description: history selection returns the smallest tool month and recommendation partition set
history projection and index regeneration are byte deterministic and idempotent
real filesystem publish set preserves append-only prefixes sealed months and coherent pointers
actual legacy corpus maps all actual rows (derived count) with exact hashes timestamps windows and explicit gaps
duplicate event changed prefix sealed edit malformed line and index mismatch fail closed
Load: a canonical 31-day four-run cadence stays bounded to 124 authoritative tool references
Regression: one tool current and monthly history resolve without unrelated narrative reads
Regression: duplicate projection and index rebuild preserve authoritative append-only history
Regression: all actual legacy rows (derived count) migrate with immutable source bytes and no invented narratives
check mode proves migration parity and immutable source bytes without writing
current pointers objects partitions indexes rows and compatibility outputs reconcile

supersededScopes.scopeName: Ordered Scheduler and Atomic Git Publication

tests.description: run state machine permits only the designed refresh author final publish commit push order
final validation requires all 18 coverage rows and provenance-preserving group decisions
scheduler publishes one exact run through isolated worktree commit and temporary remote
Canary: final author cannot execute before all 17 source outcomes validate
tool final budget input and publish faults preserve prior pointer bytes
duplicate concurrent commit and push retries remain idempotent
dirty root and remote overlap remain untouched and force an explicit refusal
Stress: concurrent duplicate scheduler attempts produce one authoritative run
Regression: refresh tools then author all briefs then final then atomic publish then commit and push
Regression: partial run failure leaves every prior current pointer and projection authoritative
Regression: duplicate and push-only retries reuse one run commit while preserving unrelated dirty work
the complete published run artifact graph reconciles

supersededScopes.scopeName: Shared Brief and History UI with Complete Delivery Integration

tests.description: shared renderer emits truthful labels escaped text safe links and distinct clocks
static loader verifies coherent pointers and fetches only selected history partitions
Canary: existing tool controls and Simple Power state survive shared brief mounts
Regression: every source tool page renders one truthful current brief section
Regression: Market Brief renders complete coverage provenance merges and preserved conflicts
Regression: tool history fetches one selected month without unrelated narratives
Regression: failed run history never replaces the last coherent current brief
Regression: recommendation timeline retains original terms and unsuccessful outcomes
Regression: all brief and history controls remain keyboard accessible and non-overlapping on mobile
Regression: authored markup renders as text and unsafe protocols never navigate
Regression: valid registry addition gains read brief history coverage and UI mount without hand-maintained lists
compatibility and consumer trace contains zero stale mutable-history assumptions
all existing product behavior remains green after all-page integration
current history final and UI-consumed artifact graph is coherent
all actual legacy rows (derived count) remain accounted for and source bytes unchanged
